title: sdk: distinguish process_runtime_error from effect_failed + first-class recovery CLI
summary: Implement durable SDK support for process-code exceptions as PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR events, keep task effect failures represented by EFFECT_RESOLVED status error, and add a conservative run:recover-process-error CLI workflow that avoids manual journal surgery.
reuseAuditFindings: No migrations, API routes, or new environment variables are expected for this SDK/runtime issue.
packages/sdk/src/runtime/orchestrateIteration.ts already returns transient process-error for non-RunFailedError exceptions.
packages/sdk/src/runtime/commitEffectResult.ts already records task outcomes as EFFECT_RESOLVED status ok or error.
packages/sdk/src/cli/commands/runIterate.ts maps process-error to failed CLI output without a durable journal event.
packages/sdk/src/mcp/tools/runs.ts already exposes process-error as recoverable.
packages/sdk/src/cli/main/runCreate.ts already owns run:rebuild-state and run:repair-journal and should host targeted recovery command logic.
vitest is already available; no dependency addition is planned.
acceptanceFocus: A process function exception records a durable PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR journal event rather than being only a transient process-error result.
PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR includes serialized error details, iteration metadata, run id or process id when available, journal head, and best-effort last effect context.
Task failures posted through task:post --status error remain EFFECT_RESOLVED status error and are not reported as process_runtime_error.
RunFailedError and infrastructure failures continue to use RUN_FAILED where appropriate.
run:iterate, run:status, run:events, MCP run tools, and renderers expose process runtime errors distinctly from effect failures.

run:recover-process-error <runDir> supports --dry-run and --json, identifies the latest typed process error, optionally patches a task result artifact, clears or supersedes only that typed marker, rebuilds state, and leaves the run ready for run:iterate.
Recovery without fixing the bad task result is honest: the marker can be cleared, but the next iterate rethrows and records a new PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR.
Malformed recovery arguments fail without mutating journal or task artifacts.
Docs and recovery prompt templates describe the new CLI workflow and stop recommending manual journal entry deletion as the default.
nonGoals: Do not implement generic journal surgery beyond the targeted process-error recovery flow.
Do not redesign effect result posting or shell task status semantics.
Do not implement defineProcess({ onProcessError }) unless maintainers explicitly pull it into scope.
Do not broaden the change into unrelated observer or TUI redesign unless required to avoid misclassification.
Do not add shell subtasks to this process definition.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #573, labels, all comments, and related issues #572, #191, and #132 to lock acceptance criteria and non-goals.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Map existing transient process-error handling, EFFECT_RESOLVED error semantics, run state derivation, CLI command registration, MCP output, docs, and recovery prompt templates.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime and recovery contract
plannedPhases.purpose: Define the PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR event payload, recovery command grammar, state model, CLI/MCP output, compatibility plan, and implementation order.

plannedPhases.name: Regression coverage
plannedPhases.purpose: Write failing tests first for durable process-error journaling, effect-failure separation, recovery happy path, honest rethrow, malformed recovery inputs, and CLI/MCP/status output.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement event journaling, state/replay consumers, run:iterate/status/events/MCP classification, run:recover-process-error, command registration, docs, and prompt updates.

plannedPhases.name: Verification gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted runtime/CLI/MCP tests, SDK build and test suites, metadata verification, and diff hygiene checks.

plannedPhases.name: Review gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Review journal invariants, recovery mutation safety, effect-vs-process classification, CLI compatibility, missing tests, and unrelated churn.

plannedPhases.name: Documentation gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm the recovery workflow is documented and targeted CLI recovery replaces manual journal deletion guidance.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm every acceptance point, quality gate, changed-file boundary, release-note candidate, and residual risk.

breakpoints.when: Only if the architecture phase finds a genuine maintainer contract decision, such as PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR terminal-state semantics or recovery patch grammar.
Only if final acceptance finds a blocking contract or verification concern that cannot be resolved within the implementation loop.

qualityGates: Typed PROCESS_RUNTIME_ERROR journaling is covered by integration tests.
Effect failure remains represented by EFFECT_RESOLVED status error and is covered separately.
Recovery CLI dry-run, JSON output, patch success, no-patch honest rethrow, malformed input failure, and no-marker failure paths are covered.
State cache, replay, status, events, and MCP consumers classify the new event consistently.
Docs and recovery prompts explain the first-class workflow and remove unsafe default journal surgery guidance.
Code review approves journal mutation safety and backward compatibility.
